private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
Fossa Systems is a Series A-stage Space & Aerospace company that has raised €15M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable set spanning five public-market participants (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and eight private peers including ClearSpace, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin, providing context across both trading and primary market benchmarks.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach, anchoring on five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables. These peers—Aalyria ($1.3B Series B), Stoke Space ($1.0B Series B), ClearSpace ($400M Series A), Hubble Network ($400M Series B), and Sift ($274M Series B)—yield a median valuation of $400M with a range of $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 45.3x across six tickers) is not applicable due to absence of target revenue disclosure, a common constraint in private company analysis.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, with a central estimate of $400M. This median aligns precisely with same-stage peer ClearSpace and reflects the lower quartile of the broader Series B cohort, appropriate for an early-stage operator in the sector. Key uncertainties include Fossa's revenue profile and near-term growth trajectory, which would refine positioning within the identified range and against the wider $274M–$1.3B peer distribution.
